verses.text: Babylon's envoys bring letters and a gift, and Hezekiah proudly shows them all his treasures. Flattered, he displays his wealth. Prosperity and praise can puff up even a godly man into a careless boast.

Babylon's envoys bring letters and a gift, and Hezekiah proudly shows them all his treasures. Flattered, he displays his wealth. Prosperity and praise can puff up even a godly man into a careless boast.
Isaiah asks what they saw; Hezekiah admits, 'all... have they seen.' He hid nothing from them. The vanity of the moment opened the door to future ruin.
Isaiah asks what they saw; Hezekiah admits, 'all... have they seen.' He hid nothing from them. The vanity of the moment opened the door to future ruin.
Isaiah foretells that all this, and the king's own sons, shall be carried to Babylon. The boast bears bitter fruit. What we proudly display we may one day lose.
Isaiah foretells that all this, and the king's own sons, shall be carried to Babylon. The boast bears bitter fruit. What we proudly display we may one day lose.
Isaiah foretells that all this, and the king's own sons, shall be carried to Babylon. The boast bears bitter fruit. What we proudly display we may one day lose.

Hezekiah answers, 'Good is the word of the LORD... for there shall be peace... in my days.' He submits, yet thinks chiefly of himself. We should care for the generations that follow, not only our own ease.
